replication_study.hypothesis: Among preschool teachers, higher weekend relaxation (REQ-Relax index, measured Sunday evening – Time 2) will positively predict joviality at the end of the subsequent work-week (PANAS-X Joviality index, measured the following Friday – Time 3), controlling for baseline joviality (Time 1) and demographic covariates.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: k17_processed_data.csv  (derived from raw files k17_t1_data.csv, k17_t2_data.csv, k17_t3_data.csv)
data_plan.source_type: Longitudinal self-report survey (Recovery Experience Questionnaire + PANAS-X)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Three-wave panel: T1 (Friday pre-weekend), T2 (Sunday evening), T3 (following Friday); all participants who completed every wave.
data_plan.sample_size: N = 339 teachers with complete three-wave data (see dataset shape).
data_plan.access_details: Fully anonymised CSV files supplied in /app/data/replication_data; no additional permissions required.

qualification.explanation: The dataset replicates the original survey design (same measurement occasions, instruments, and occupational population) but is collected independently, giving a fresh sample for robustness testing.
qualification.similarity_to_original: 1. Uses identical instruments: REQ items req_relax_1–4 and PANAS-X joviality items (Data_Code_Book.csv; post_registration.json ‘Recovery Experience Questionnaire and PANAS-X’).  2. Same timing structure: T1 Friday, T2 Sunday, T3 next Friday (k17_data_prep__py.py logic lines 153-170; post_registration.json ‘Three measurement points’).  3. Same population – preschool/early-childhood educators (initial_details.txt, post_registration.json; variable work_years in Data_Code_Book.csv).
qualification.deviation_from_original: 1. Larger & later sample (339 vs 229; get_dataset_shape result) collected in 2021 (age derived with anchor year 2021 in k17_data_prep__py.py line 232), whereas original data were collected earlier (year not specified in paper). 2. Participants drawn from new recruitment waves (Wave variable computed in data prep) rather than the cohort analysed in the original study. 3. Minor coding differences: gender coded 1 = male, 2 = female here (Data_Code_Book.csv) whereas original coding scheme not stated.

data_plan.notes: Variables required for the focal regression are present (req_relax, T3_panas_joviality, T1_panas_joviality, age, gender, children, work_hours, work_days, hassles). Processed dataset already contains composite indices created exactly as in the original R code. No obvious encoding problems; listwise deletion will be applied.

planned_method.steps:
1. Execute k17_data_prep__py.py to (a) load raw wave CSVs, (b) build composite indices, (c) produce k17_processed_data.csv and (d) automatically invoke k17_analysis__py.py.
2. k17_analysis__py.py loads processed data, standardises continuous predictors, and fits hierarchical OLS regression.
3. Extract coefficient, t-statistic and p-value for req_relax.
4. Save full regression table to /app/data/replication_results.csv.
planned_method.models: Hierarchical multiple linear regression (ordinary least squares). Focal model equals Model_3 in original R code.
planned_method.outcome_variable: T3_panas_joviality (mean of eight joviality items, Time 3).
planned_method.independent_variables: req_relax (mean of four relaxation items, Time 2).
planned_method.control_variables: age, gender (categorical), children (binary), work_hours, work_days, T1_panas_joviality (baseline affect), req_control, req_mastery, req_detach, hassles.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pandas, numpy, scikit-learn (for scaling), statsmodels (for regression).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Standardised regression coefficient for req_relax in the full model.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: One-tailed t-test on regression coefficient (H₁: β > 0) with α = 0.05.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion on variables used in the focal regression (implemented in k17_analysis__py.py).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Successful replication if coefficient on req_relax is positive and p < 0.05 (one-tailed). Effect magnitude compared with original β ≈ 0.15.

files.k17_data_prep__py.py: Transforms raw survey CSVs (T1-T3) into analytic dataset: merges waves, computes REQ & PANAS composites, derives demographics, writes /app/data/replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv and then dispatches analysis.
files.k17_analysis__py.py: Loads processed data, scales predictors, fits hierarchical OLS regression replicating original Model 3, prints summary, and saves regression output to /app/data/replication_results.csv.

codebase.notes: All file IO conforms to /app/data policy. Original R scripts (k17_analysis.R, k17_data_prep.R) are retained for reference but are not executed; their logic has been faithfully translated into Python.

analysis.instructions:
In container shell:  
python /app/data/replication_data/k17_data_prep__py.py  
This produces processed data and executes k17_analysis__py.py automatically; results appear in /app/data/replication_results.csv and console output.
analysis.comparison_metrics:
1. Sign and statistical significance of β(req_relax).
2. Magnitude comparison: replicated β vs original 0.15.
3. Confidence/p-value alignment (<0.05 one-tailed).
